lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: As the Darkness fills up in the void, she felt a sudden right beam towards her direction.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: A light? Where am I...?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Is this another dream?

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Curious from the beam, Elsa follows where the light shines.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: At the other side of the light, She sees her mother crying and wailing below the statue of Mother Mary.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Oh Holy Mary, Mother of God. Has my vocation as a woman failed in your eyes?

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: Why must I be the outlet of my husband’s treachery?

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: His lust... His rage... And why must my children be the fruit of his sin?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: 'His lust... His rage... ?'

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: From the darkness behind her, a hand grabs Elsa’s neck.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Hah... God... Please...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Who's... Choking me...

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: When she looks where her Mother is, she's not there.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: But she feels her warmth. The hand on her neck grips harder, strangling her.

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.text: The fruit of sin shall rot and never bear seed!

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: She wakes up in a cold sweat on her face.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Hah... Hah...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: What...? Where am I now?

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Looking around her, she noticed multiple Holy Crosses around the room.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: As the Mother Mary figures slowly appear, looking down at her with empty eyes.

lines.speaker: Voices
lines.text: You are the fruit of pain. You are the fruit of suffering.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: No...

lines.speaker: Voices
lines.text: And the fruit shall rot. Atone for your existence.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: No... No...

lines.speaker: Voices
lines.text: And the fruit shall rot.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Make it stop.. I’ve had enough...

lines.speaker: Voices
lines.text: You are the fruit which bears the rotten seed of sin.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: STOP!!!

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Looking around the room, she looked the drawers, and dresser, hoping to find her pills.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: She found them beside a very sharp edged cross, shaped like a knife.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: What will she do?

choices.text: Breaks all the Statues and Crosses in the room

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Overlapping voices echoed in her mind as her sanity unraveled.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Gripping the sharpened cross, she violently decapitated the statues and shattered every crucifix in sight.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: As the voices began to fade, she looked down... her hands soaked in blood.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: What?... No... What have I done... I...

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Overwhelmed by confusion and regret, she dropped the cross from her hand and swallowed most of the pills she had.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Desperate to silence everything... she began to cough up blood.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: End of Chapter 3.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Overlapping voices echoed in her mind as her sanity unraveled.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Gripping the sharpened cross... she turned it inward.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Again, and again, she stabbed herself, desperate to make it stop.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: As the voices dimmed, she stared at her trembling, bloody hands...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: What?... No... What have I done... I...

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Numb and confused, she collapsed to the ground and swallowed most of the pills she had.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Her body trembled as the blood came pouring from her lips.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: End of Chapter 3.
